text: The surgeon performs a thymectomy using three small incisions in the chest, employing ultrasonic shears to gently dissect and remove the thymus and tumor without burning the tissue. The heart is visible, pumping beneath the surgical field, highlighting the need for careful maneuvering around the tumor.

The surgeon carefully dissects the thymus gland, revealing a large vein adjacent to the glandular structure. The small veins are meticulously managed to prevent excessive bleeding during the thoracoscopic thymectomy procedure.
